name: Apple iPad Air 13-inch (M3)
introText: The iPad Air 13-inch with M3 is the largest iPad Air ever, offering near-Pro performance in a 13-inch Liquid Retina display for $1,099 CAD — the best large-format iPad for most users.
whatWorks: M3 chip makes the iPad Air as fast as a MacBook Air for most tasks — handles video editing in Premiere Rush, illustration in Procreate, and multitasking without hesitation.
13-inch size is ideal for artists and productivity users who want an iPad without paying for the Pro — the extra screen real estate is transformative for drawing and side-by-side multitasking.
worthKnowing: No OLED display — the Liquid Retina LCD is very good but doesn't match the iPad Pro's Ultra Retina XDR OLED for contrast and color depth.
Apple Pencil Pro support but no ProMotion 120Hz — the display caps at 60Hz, which is noticeably less smooth than the iPad Pro for fast-stroke drawing.

commonComplaints.title: 60Hz display lags behind iPad Pro
commonComplaints.body: The iPad Air's 60Hz display is less smooth for fast drawing and scrolling compared to the iPad Pro's ProMotion 120Hz. Artists who use the Apple Pencil heavily will notice this.

commonComplaints.title: LCD vs. OLED is visible
commonComplaints.body: The LCD display shows a slightly gray black vs. the iPad Pro OLED's true black. Not an issue for productivity, but visible for media consumption.

commonComplaints.title: Heavy at 617g for extended hand-holding
commonComplaints.body: The 13-inch size and 617g weight make it less comfortable to hold in one hand for reading or browsing. Best used flat or on a stand.

verdict: The iPad Air 13 M3 is the best large-format iPad for users who don't need the Pro's OLED or 120Hz display — it matches the iPad Pro on performance and app support at $500 less. If you're a professional artist who draws every day, the iPad Pro 13's display and ProMotion justify the premium.

realBuyers.quote: Replaced my iPad Pro 11 with this. The 13-inch screen for drawing is a game changer. Performance is identical.
Use it for note-taking in law school. The size is perfect. Battery lasts a full day of lectures.
Video editing on the go is seamless. Can't believe this isn't the Pro for how fast it is.

sources.title: iPad Air M3 Review — The Verge

metaDescription: Apple iPad Air 13 M3 review Canada: M3 chip, 13" Liquid Retina, 128GB. $1,099 CAD. Best large-format iPad for most Canadian users.
